summary.news_summary: 【テストモード】APIキーが設定されていないため、実際の要約は生成されていません。GEMINI_API_KEY環境変数を設定してください。収集された記事のタイトルのみ表示しています。

raw_articles.title: Best Microsoft Surface Laptop (2026): Which Model to Buy or Avoid
raw_articles.summary: With pricing in flux and new models available, here’s which Surface Laptop and Surface Pro to buy.

raw_articles.title: Influencers Are Promoting $50 Straws They Claim Will Protect Against Electromagnetic Radiation
raw_articles.summary: “EMF straws” and similar products are being sold as a way to block electromagnetic frequencies that come from common electronic devices, even without scientific evidence that they work.

raw_articles.title: OpenAI Fixes 18-Year-Old GNU libunwind Bug by Treating Crash Debugging Like Epidemiology
raw_articles.summary: OpenAI found two unrelated bugs masquerading as one in ChatGPT's data infrastructure. Silent hardware corruption on one Azure host and an 18-year-old race condition in GNU libunwind's setcontext function with a one-instruction vulnerability window. The breakthrough came from switching to population-level crash analysis rather than examining individual core dumps. By Steef-Jan Wiggers

raw_articles.title: The $28 Million Mistake That Inspired Estonia's AI “Fuckup Finder”
raw_articles.summary: A single wording mistake cost the government millions. Now Estonia is using AI to spot legal errors before they become law—and to automate more of the state.

raw_articles.title: Meta's new AI feature lets people create images from your Instagram posts - how to opt out
raw_articles.summary: Any public Instagram account is fair game to be used for AI generation with Meta's new Muse Image.

raw_articles.title: Best blood pressure watches: I tested the top models that actually work
raw_articles.summary: I tested some of the top blood pressure watches available, and considered medical research and current FDA guidance, to help you choose the best one for your needs.

raw_articles.title: What AI companies want for the millions they're spending on elections
raw_articles.summary: Lawmakers are working on AI legislation — and two major industry PACs are each pushing for their own version of regulation.

raw_articles.title: Federal Investigators Say Certain DOGE Records Were Deleted
raw_articles.summary: A government report claims DOGE didn’t access sensitive systems. It also says the agency deleted records that would show if they had.

raw_articles.summary: It's too hot. There, we said it. Protect your health and keep your home cool with one of these top-rated air conditioners.

raw_articles.title: AirPods-maker Luxshare sees shares close lower in tepid Hong Kong debut
raw_articles.summary: The company, which is already listed in Shenzhen, had priced shares in the IPO at 63.28 Hong Kong dollars apiece, raising HK$24.27 billion ($3.09 billion).

raw_articles.title: AlloyDB Ships Proxy Models That Replace LLM Calls with Local Inference Inside the Database
raw_articles.summary: Google shipped AlloyDB AI functions GA with a proxy model architecture that trains a lightweight local model from LLM outputs, then runs queries at database speed without external calls. Smart batching delivers 2,400x throughput improvement. The proxy model reaches 100,000 rows per second in preview, but benchmark numbers apply only to ai.if in internal testing. By Steef-Jan Wiggers

raw_articles.title: Meta’s Muse Image Might be Just What SMBs Need
raw_articles.summary: Meta’s new AI imaging model enables users to create competitive ad content on the social media giant’s platforms.

raw_articles.title: Jim Cramer sees a big risk to the bull market resurfacing — and it's not the Iran war
raw_articles.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said the growing wave of stock offerings and debt issuance is the next big threat to the bull market.

raw_articles.title: SpaceX's Grok 4.5 launches at half the price of rivals — here's why that could rattle Anthropic and OpenAI
raw_articles.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX released Grok 4.5 on Wednesday, the first artificial intelligence model the company has trained specifically for coding and autonomous agents — and the first tangible product of its $60 billion acquisition of the AI coding startup Cursor, completed just weeks ago.The launch marks a pivotal test of the sprawling, vertically integrated AI empire Musk has assembled over the past six months, and of a strategy that bets developers care less about topping benchmark leaderboards
